TD Distribute
← Studio Live site ↗
Platforms
Discovery
Automation
Validation
Reddit

Post to your subreddit (r/ThinkersDialemma or similar). Reddit users respond best to text posts with a genuine excerpt — not just a link drop.

Post title
Post body
Setup
1

Create your subreddit

Go to reddit.com/subreddits/create and create r/ThinkersDialemma. Set it to public, add a description and banner.

2

Post each essay manually

Use the formatted text above. Post as a "Text" post, not a link — Reddit's algorithm favors native content. Include the Substack URL at the end.

3

Automate with Zapier (optional)

See the Zapier / Make panel. You can auto-post each new RSS item as a Reddit link post to your subreddit.

4

Reddit API (fully automated)

Create a Reddit app at reddit.com/prefs/apps. Use the script type, note your client_id and client_secret, then add them to GitHub Secrets and the provided GitHub Actions workflow.

Facebook Page

Facebook's algorithm amplifies native video and long-form text. Share as a Page post — paste the excerpt and link; let Facebook generate the link preview.

Page post
Setup
1

Create a Facebook Page

Go to facebook.com/pages/create. Choose "Public Figure" or "Media/News". Name it "Thinkers Dilemma".

2

Post manually

Paste the formatted text above into a new post. Paste the Substack URL — Facebook generates the preview automatically. Delete the URL from the post text once the preview loads.

3

Automate with Meta Business Suite

Go to business.facebook.com → Content → RSS Feed. Add https://thinkersdilemma.substack.com/feed. Meta will auto-post new items to your Page.

4

Automate with Zapier

Trigger: RSS Feed (new item). Action: Facebook Pages → Create Page Post. Maps title + excerpt + link automatically. See the Zapier panel for full setup.

LinkedIn

LinkedIn rewards "document" posts and thoughtful text posts — not just link shares. The formatted post below is styled for maximum organic reach.

LinkedIn post
Setup
1

Create a LinkedIn Page

Go to linkedin.com/company/setup/new. Create "Thinkers Dilemma" as a Media company page.

2

Post manually

Post as yourself or as the Page. Paste the formatted text, then add the Substack URL as a first comment (not in the post body — this improves organic reach).

3

Automate with Zapier

Trigger: RSS Feed. Action: LinkedIn → Create Company Update or Share Post. The Zapier LinkedIn integration maps RSS fields to post fields automatically.

4

LinkedIn API

Create an app at linkedin.com/developers/apps. Request the w_member_social scope. Store tokens in GitHub Secrets. The GitHub Actions workflow can then post on each sync.

Medium

Medium has a built-in RSS import that creates drafts from your Substack feed automatically — no API needed for basic use.

Medium subtitle / lede
Setup
1

Import via RSS (recommended)

Go to medium.com/me/import. Enter https://thinkersdilemma.substack.com/feed. Medium imports all posts as drafts — you review and publish each one.

2

Create a Medium Publication

Set up "Thinkers Dilemma" as a Medium publication. This lets readers follow the publication independently of your personal account.

3

Add canonical URL

In Medium's story settings, set the canonical URL to the Substack post URL. This tells Google the Substack version is the primary source — prevents duplicate content penalties.

4

Medium API (fully automated)

Get your integration token at medium.com/me/settings. Add it to GitHub Secrets as MEDIUM_TOKEN. The GitHub Actions workflow can auto-publish on each RSS sync.

RSS Aggregators & Discovery

Your Substack RSS feed is already live and valid. Submit it to major aggregators — readers who use RSS readers will automatically receive every new essay.

Your RSS feed URL
https://thinkersdilemma.substack.com/feed
Submit to these aggregators
+ Feedly + Inoreader Flipboard ↗ Pocket ↗ NewsBlur ↗ The Old Reader ↗
✓

Feedly & Inoreader — one click

The links above open pre-filled subscription pages. Users who click "Follow on Substack" or subscribe to your Feedly source auto-receive new essays.

✓

Google Discover / Search

Substack generates a valid sitemap.xml and RSS. Google indexes these automatically. No action needed.

→

Flipboard

Create a Flipboard account, then go to Profile → Manage Magazines and add your RSS URL as a source magazine. Flipboard will surface posts to its 100M+ user base.

Zapier / Make — Automated Cross-Posting

Set up once. Every time you publish on Substack, all connected platforms update automatically — no manual work. Zapier and Make.com are the simplest paths.

Zapier

~$20 / month

5 Zaps for 5 platforms. Easiest setup — no code, visual editor, pre-built Substack + Reddit + LinkedIn + Facebook + Medium integrations.

zapier.com ↗

Make (Integromat)

~$10 / month

More powerful than Zapier. One "Scenario" can handle all 5 platforms in a single flow. Better for custom formatting per platform.

make.com ↗

n8n (self-hosted)

Free (self-hosted)

Open-source Zapier alternative. Host on Railway or Render for ~$5/mo. Full control, no usage limits. Steeper setup than Zapier.

n8n.io ↗

GitHub Actions

Free

Already set up in your project. Add platform API keys to GitHub Secrets and posting scripts — runs every 6 hours alongside the RSS sync.

See GitHub Actions panel ↗
Zapier setup — step by step
1

Create a Zapier account

Go to zapier.com and sign up. The Starter plan (~$20/mo) covers 5 Zaps and 750 tasks/month — enough for weekly essays across 5 platforms.

2

Create Zap 1 — Trigger

Click "Create Zap". Trigger app: RSS by Zapier. Event: New Item in Feed. Feed URL: https://thinkersdilemma.substack.com/feed. Test — Zapier fetches your latest post.

3

Add an Action for each platform

Add an Action step for each platform. Map RSS fields: title → Post Title, description → Post Body, link → URL. You can add a Filter step to only trigger for essays (not podcast-only posts) by checking if the title contains certain keywords.

4

Platform-specific actions

Reddit: "Create Post" → subreddit, title, URL.   Facebook: "Create Page Post" → message + link.   LinkedIn: "Create Company Update".   Medium: "Create Post" using the Medium integration (requires your integration token from medium.com/me/settings).

5

Test & turn on

Test each action with the latest post. Zapier shows you exactly what will be sent. Turn the Zap on — from now on, every new Substack post triggers all 5 platform actions within ~15 minutes of publishing.

GitHub Actions — Free Automation

Your project already has .github/workflows/sync-rss.yml running every 6 hours. You can extend it to also cross-post to each platform using their APIs — free, no third-party services needed.

1

Push your project to GitHub

If you haven't already: create a repo, git init, add your files, push. The .github/workflows/ folder will activate automatically.

2

Create platform API credentials

Reddit: reddit.com/prefs/apps → create "script" app. LinkedIn: linkedin.com/developers/apps → request w_member_social. Facebook: developers.facebook.com → create app, get Page token. Medium: medium.com/me/settings → Integration tokens.

3

Add secrets to GitHub

In your repo: Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions. Add each credential as a secret: REDDIT_CLIENT_ID, REDDIT_SECRET, REDDIT_USERNAME, REDDIT_PASSWORD, LINKEDIN_TOKEN, FACEBOOK_PAGE_TOKEN, FACEBOOK_PAGE_ID, MEDIUM_TOKEN.

4

Add posting scripts

The existing scripts/sync-rss-node.js already detects new essays. Add posting functions to it or create separate scripts — one per platform. Each script: fetch the latest essay from the RSS feed, POST to the platform API using the stored secrets.

5

Update the workflow

In .github/workflows/sync-rss.yml, add a step after the RSS sync: node scripts/post-to-platforms.js. Gate it with if: steps.sync.outputs.new_content == 'true' so it only runs when there's actually a new essay — avoiding duplicate posts.

Status & Validation

Live view of dist/status.json — written by GitHub Actions after every sync and cross-post run.
